issues.title: API: add iter_paged() generator at the client level

tests.scope: Drive client.iter_paged(...) directly against a live SANDBOX list endpoint to confirm the new public method exists with the documented signature, walks pages on demand, honours max_records as a hard cap, and yields dict records that match the Alma /acq/invoices payload shape.
passCriteria: iter_paged is callable with the documented kwargs (endpoint, record_key, page_size, max_records) without raising TypeError or AlmaValidationError.
result is a list (the test consumes the generator via list(...)).
len(result) is <= 10 (max_records cap is respected; the SANDBOX may legitimately have fewer than 10 invoices, in which case the cap is not the limiting factor and the smaller result is acceptable).
Every element of result is a dict (the per-record payload yielded under record_key='invoice').
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised across the walk.

tests.scope: Confirm the migrated Acquisitions.list_invoices proof-point method still produces a valid response against live SANDBOX data and preserves its pre-#11 public return shape ({'invoice': [...], 'total_record_count': N}) so existing callers continue to work bit-for-bit.
passCriteria: result is a dict (the legacy Alma list-payload shape is preserved).
result contains the key 'invoice' and result['invoice'] is a list.
len(result['invoice']) is <= 5 (the limit cap from the call is honoured).
Every element of result['invoice'] is a dict.
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised.

tests.scope: Confirm the migrated Acquisitions.search_invoices proof-point method still produces valid results against live SANDBOX data after being rewired to client.iter_paged. Replaces the original /bibs-based test: GET /almaws/v1/bibs is identifier-only and rejects q= queries (HTTP 400, errorCode 401873), so it cannot validate iter_paged() against a paginated search. /acq/invoices genuinely supports q= + offset pagination, which is what iter_paged() is for. BibliographicRecords.search_records was removed entirely in the same change (it never worked at the live API).
passCriteria: result is a dict (the migrated method preserves the legacy Alma list payload shape).
result['invoice'] is a list capped at <= 5 elements (the limit cap is honoured; SANDBOX may legitimately return fewer hits, including zero).
Every element of result['invoice'] is a dict.
No AlmaAPIError, AlmaValidationError, or AlmaRateLimitError is raised.

unmappable.reason: The 'tests still pass' facet refers to the local pytest suite (18 unit tests across tests/unit/client/test_alma_api_client.py, tests/unit/domains/test_acquisitions.py, tests/unit/domains/test_bibs.py) which is exercised pre-merge in CI / local environment, not a SANDBOX behaviour. The live-API facet of AC-2 (the migrated methods still produce valid results) is mapped to t-11-2 and t-11-3.

unmappable.ac: AC-3: Documented in CLAUDE.md as the preferred way to walk results.
unmappable.reason: Doc-only acceptance criterion. CLAUDE.md is a local repo file; its content cannot be observed via Alma SANDBOX API behaviour. Verified by reviewing the CLAUDE.md diff in the chunk PR, not by live calls.

unmappable.ac: AC-4: `_fetch_all_pages()` references in CLAUDE.md / python-dev-expert skill aligned.
unmappable.reason: Doc-only acceptance criterion. References inside CLAUDE.md and the python-dev-expert skill text are not observable via SANDBOX API behaviour. Verified by reviewing the CLAUDE.md diff and skill text in the chunk PR.

unmappable.ac: AC-5: Generator semantics (yield, not list) preserved — callers that need a list use `list(...)`.
unmappable.reason: Generator-vs-list semantics is a Python language-level property of the iter_paged return value (e.g., inspect.isgenerator(...) / lazy fetch verification). It is not observable as Alma API behaviour and is more naturally and reliably exercised as a unit test (already covered in tests/unit/client/test_alma_api_client.py). A SANDBOX smoke could only re-confirm what the unit suite already proves, so it is intentionally not duplicated here.
